title: arctostaphylos uva-ursi subsp. hookeri
intro: Arctostaphylos uva-ursi subsp. hookeri, also known as kinnikinnick, is a subspecies of the bearberry plant, a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its antiseptic, astringent, and antifungal properties, which make it effective in treating urinary tract infections, diarrhea, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Arctostaphylos uva-ursi subsp. hookeri include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
